data.title: As the sprint task execution agent, I consume the shared behavioral testing standard so my inline tests resist refactoring
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the sprint task execution sub-agent prompt to reference and consume the shared behavioral testing standard when writing tests inline during task implementation.
**Why**: Sprint task execution currently writes tests without the shared standard, leading to change-detector tests that break on refactoring.
**Scope**:
- IN: Sprint task execution prompt references the shared behavioral testing standard
- IN: Review resolution prompt references the shared behavioral testing standard
- OUT: Sprint orchestrator routing changes (separate story)
- OUT: Modifying the shared testing standard itself (separate story)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, sprint task execution and review resolution prompts consume the shared behavioral testing standard
  ← Satisfies: "All test-writing agent prompts (RED test writer, fix-bug, sprint task execution, review resolution) consume a shared behavioral testing standard"
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD done-of-done

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
